text:
{
  "english": {
    "IsA": [
      "It is a Mughal miniature painting.",
      "It is a painting for the court of the Fourth Mughal emperor Jahangir."
    ],
    "HasA": [
      "It has a depiction of the emperor Jahangir.",
      "It has a throne in the form of an hourglass.",
      "It has a Sufi saint.",
      "It has the Ottoman sultan looking on.",
      "It has the king of England looking on.",
      "It has the artist Bichitr pictured in the bottom-left corner.",
      "It has a halo around the emperor's face.",
      "It has a Persian inscription on the throne.",
      "It has inscriptions in the margins.",
      "It has a Venetian-styled carpet in the lower half of the background."
    ],
    "HasFeatureOf": [
      "It is characterized by hierarchical proportion.",
      "It is characterized by a self-insert of the artist.",
      "It is characterized by a halo combining the imagery of the sun and the crescent moon.",
      "It is characterized by a throne shaped like a European hourglass.",
      "It is characterized by a Kavuk turban worn by the Ottoman sultan.",
      "It is characterized by European style attire worn by James VI.",
      "It is characterized by a Hindu-styled robe worn by the artist.",
      "It is characterized by angel motifs.",
      "It is characterized by cupid like child angels.",
      "It is characterized by a Christian cross worn by Jahangir."
    ],
    "UsedFor": [
      "It is used for depicting the emperor handing a book to a Sufi saint.",
      "It is used for presenting the emperor with his status of ruler.",
      "It is used for indicating that the painting was presented to the emperor on his birthday.",
      "It is used for conveying the message that the emperor prefers gathering with holy men compared to visits from a court of kings.",
      "It is used for interpreting Jahangir preferring Shaikh Hussain over fellow rulers.",
      "It is used for signifying Jahangir's reverence towards the saint.",
      "It is used for presenting Bichitr as a prophet who exposes the hidden truth about divine order.",
      "It is used for showing the Mughals leaders extension of power and diplomacy beyond his own religion."
    ],
    "LocatedIn": [
      "It is located in the Freer Gallery of Art."
    ],
    "BuiltIn": [
      "It was built in c. 1615–1618."
    ],
    "BuiltBy": [
      "It was built by Bichitr."
    ],
    "OriginatedIn": [
      "It originated in the Mughal court."
    ],
    "FoundedBy": [
      "unknown"
    ],
    "FoundedIn": [
      "unknown"
    ],
    "CreatedBy": [
      "It was created by Bichitr."
    ],
    "CreatedIn": [
      "It was created in c. 1615–1618."
    ],

      "unknown"

    "ProducedBy": [
      "It was produced by Bichitr."
    ],
    "CausedBy": [
      "unknown"
    ],
    "Affects": [
      "unknown"
    ],
    "ConsistsOf": [
      "It consists of figures of the emperor, a Sufi saint, the Ottoman sultan, the king of England, and the artist.",
      "It consists of a throne shaped like an hourglass.",
      "It consists of a halo around the emperor's face.",
      "It consists of inscriptions.",
      "It consists of a Venetian-styled carpet.",
      "It consists of sky blue in the upper half of the background.",
      "It consists of four putti.",
      "It consists of Persian couplets.",
      "It consists of angel motifs.",
      "It consists of a Christian cross."
    ],
    "PartOf": [
      "unknown"
    ],
    "MemberOf": [
      "unknown"
    ],
    "BelongsTo": [
      "It belongs to the court of the Fourth Mughal emperor Jahangir."
    ],
    "KnownFor": [
      "It is known for depicting Jahangir preferring a Sufi Shaikh to Kings.",
      "It is known for its self-insert of the artist Bichitr.",
      "It is known for its symbolic representation of divinity and status.",
      "It is known for its blend of Persian and European traditions.",
      "It is known for its depiction of hierarchical proportion."
    ],
    "AssociatedWith": [
      "It is associated with the artist Bichitr.",
      "It is associated with the Mughal emperor Jahangir.",
      "It is associated with the Freer Gallery of Art.",
      "It is associated with the Sufi sect Chishti.",
      "It is associated with Giovanni Bellini.",
      "It is associated with John de Critz.",
      "It is associated with Thomas Roe.",
      "It is associated with the second Islamic millennium.",
      "It is associated with European putto.",
      "It is associated with Persian traditions."

    "NamedAfter": [
      "unknown"

    "OccurredIn": [

    "HeldIn": [

      "unknown"

    "About": [
      "It is about Jahangir preferring a Sufi Shaikh to Kings.",
      "It is about the emperor Jahangir's reverence towards holy men.",
      "It is about the status and divinity of the emperor.",
      "It is about the blending of Persian and European traditions in Mughal art.",
      "It is about Bichitr's role as an artist and prophet."
    ],
    "InfluencedBy": [
      "It was influenced by Giovanni Bellini.",
      "It was influenced by John de Critz.",
      "It was influenced by European putto.",
      "It was influenced by European hourglass imagery.",
      "It was influenced by the tradition of self-insertion in Mughal painting."

  "japanese": {
    "IsA": [
      "それはムガル朝の細密画です。",
      "それは第4代ムガル皇帝ジャハーンギールの宮廷のための絵画です。"
    ],
    "HasA": [
      "それはジャハーンギール皇帝の描写を持っています。",
      "それは砂時計の形の玉座を持っています。",
      "それはスーフィーの聖者を持っています。",
      "それはオスマン帝国のスルタンが眺めているのを持っています。",
      "それはイングランド王が眺めているのを持っています。",
      "それは画家ビチトルが左下隅に描かれているのを持っています。",
      "それは皇帝の顔の周りの光輪を持っています。",
      "それは玉座のペルシャの碑文を持っています。",
      "それは余白の碑文を持っています。",
      "それは背景の下半分にヴェネツィア風の絨毯を持っています。"
    ],
    "HasFeatureOf": [
      "それは階層的プロポーションによって特徴づけられます。",
      "それは画家の自己挿入によって特徴づけられます。",
      "それは太陽と三日月の両方のイメージを組み合わせた皇帝の顔の周りの光輪によって特徴づけられます。",
      "それはヨーロッパの砂時計の形をした玉座によって特徴づけられます。",
      "それはオスマン帝国のスルタンが着ているカヴクターバンによって特徴づけられます。",
      "それはジェームズ6世が着ているヨーロッパ風の服装によって特徴づけられます。",
      "それは画家が着ているヒンドゥー教風のローブによって特徴づけられます。",
      "それは天使のモチーフによって特徴づけられます。",
      "それはキューピッドのような子供の天使によって特徴づけられます。",
      "それはジャハーンギールが着ているキリスト教の十字架によって特徴づけられます。"
    ],
    "UsedFor": [
      "それは皇帝がスーフィーの聖者に本を渡す様子を描写するために使われます。",
      "それは皇帝の支配者としての地位を示すために使われます。",
      "それは絵画が皇帝の誕生日​​に贈られたことを示すために使われます。",
      "それは皇帝が王たちの訪問よりも聖なる人々との集まりを好むというメッセージを伝えるために使われます。",
      "それはジャハーンギールがシャイフ・フサインを仲間の支配者よりも好むと解釈するために使われます。",
      "それは聖者に対するジャハーンギールの敬意を示すために使われます。",
      "それはビチトルを、神聖な秩序に関する隠された真実を明らかにする預言者として提示するために使われます。",
      "それはムガル皇帝の自身の宗教を超えた権力と外交の拡大を示すために使われます。"
    ],
    "LocatedIn": [
      "それはフリーア美術館にあります。"
    ],
    "BuiltIn": [
      "それは1615年から1618年頃に建てられました。"
    ],
    "BuiltBy": [
      "それはビチトルによって建てられました。"
    ],

      "それはムガル朝の宮廷で生まれました。"

IsA: It is a Mughal miniature painting.
It is a painting for the court of the Fourth Mughal emperor Jahangir.
HasA: It has a depiction of the emperor Jahangir.
It has a throne in the form of an hourglass.
It has a Sufi saint.
It has the Ottoman sultan looking on.
It has the king of England looking on.
It has the artist Bichitr pictured in the bottom-left corner.
It has a halo around the emperor's face.
It has a Persian inscription on the throne.
It has inscriptions in the margins.
It has a Venetian-styled carpet in the lower half of the background.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by hierarchical proportion.
It is characterized by a self-insert of the artist.
It is characterized by a halo combining the imagery of the sun and the crescent moon.
It is characterized by a throne shaped like a European hourglass.
It is characterized by a Kavuk turban worn by the Ottoman sultan.
It is characterized by European style attire worn by James VI.
It is characterized by a Hindu-styled robe worn by the artist.
It is characterized by angel motifs.
It is characterized by cupid like child angels.
It is characterized by a Christian cross worn by Jahangir.
UsedFor: It is used for depicting the emperor handing a book to a Sufi saint.
It is used for presenting the emperor with his status of ruler.
It is used for indicating that the painting was presented to the emperor on his birthday.
It is used for conveying the message that the emperor prefers gathering with holy men compared to visits from a court of kings.
It is used for interpreting Jahangir preferring Shaikh Hussain over fellow rulers.
It is used for signifying Jahangir's reverence towards the saint.
It is used for presenting Bichitr as a prophet who exposes the hidden truth about divine order.
It is used for showing the Mughals leaders extension of power and diplomacy beyond his own religion.
LocatedIn: It is located in the Freer Gallery of Art.
OriginatedIn: It originated in the Mughal court.
ConsistsOf: It consists of figures of the emperor, a Sufi saint, the Ottoman sultan, the king of England, and the artist.
It consists of a throne shaped like an hourglass.
It consists of a halo around the emperor's face.
It consists of inscriptions.
It consists of a Venetian-styled carpet.
It consists of sky blue in the upper half of the background.
It consists of four putti.
It consists of Persian couplets.
It consists of angel motifs.
It consists of a Christian cross.
BelongsTo: It belongs to the court of the Fourth Mughal emperor Jahangir.
KnownFor: It is known for depicting Jahangir preferring a Sufi Shaikh to Kings.
It is known for its self-insert of the artist Bichitr.
It is known for its symbolic representation of divinity and status.
It is known for its blend of Persian and European traditions.
It is known for its depiction of hierarchical proportion.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with the artist Bichitr.
It is associated with the Mughal emperor Jahangir.
It is associated with the Freer Gallery of Art.
It is associated with the Sufi sect Chishti.
It is associated with Giovanni Bellini.
It is associated with John de Critz.
It is associated with Thomas Roe.
It is associated with the second Islamic millennium.
It is associated with European putto.
It is associated with Persian traditions.
HeldIn: It was held in the court of the Fourth Mughal emperor Jahangir.
About: It is about Jahangir preferring a Sufi Shaikh to Kings.
It is about the emperor Jahangir's reverence towards holy men.
It is about the status and divinity of the emperor.
It is about the blending of Persian and European traditions in Mughal art.
It is about Bichitr's role as an artist and prophet.
InfluencedBy: It was influenced by Giovanni Bellini.
It was influenced by John de Critz.
It was influenced by European putto.
It was influenced by European hourglass imagery.
It was influenced by the tradition of self-insertion in Mughal painting.
Influenced: It influenced the custom of self-insertion in Mughal painting.
